rule.name: Springkotlin Debug/Development Build Exposure
rule.rule_desc: Detects debug/development flags in Kotlin app/service code.
rule.vuln_desc: Debug-enabled builds can increase runtime information exposure.
rule.developer: Disable debuggable flags in production builds.
rule.reviewer: Validate release build config and runtime debug guards. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Springkotlin Insecure Trust Manager / TLS Bypass
rule.rule_desc: Detects TLS certificate/hostname verification bypass patterns.
rule.vuln_desc: Bypassing TLS validation can enable MITM attacks.
rule.developer: Use strict certificate and hostname verification.
rule.reviewer: Inspect HTTP client TLS configuration for bypasses. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Springkotlin SQL Injection Sink
rule.vuln_desc: Concatenated SQL may lead to injection vulnerabilities.
rule.developer: Use parameterized queries and prepared statements.
rule.reviewer: Trace untrusted input into SQL construction sites. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Springkotlin Permissive CORS/Origin
rule.vuln_desc: Wildcard origins may expose sensitive endpoints/data.
rule.reviewer: Review HTTP/security config for least privilege. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Springkotlin Hardcoded Secret Material
rule.vuln_desc: Hardcoded secrets can be extracted from source/apk artifacts.
rule.developer: Use secure config/secret stores and rotate leaked values.
rule.reviewer: Validate no production secrets are committed. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
